conbody.p: Code is not magic. It is a set of precise instructions written in a language a computer can execute. As a translator or linguist, you already work with language rules — Python is simply another rule-based system.

section.title: A Useful Analogy
section.p: Think of a Python script the same way you think of a macro in Word or Excel: a saved sequence of actions that automates repetitive work. You do not need to write the macro yourself — you only need to run it.

example.title: Example: What a Script Actually Does
example.p: A term-extraction script does the following three things in order:

ol.li: Reads every sentence and identifies noun phrases
Writes the results to a new file

example.p: Each line of code is one instruction in that sequence.
